module_doc:
BUG: Download page does not strip old-format SGMETA envelope.

Observed at: dev.send.sgraph.ai/en-gb/download/#help-aunt-2780 (31 March 2026)
Fixed in: v0.20.29 (commit a28e8206 — "handle old SGMETA wire format")

Root cause:
  Two SGMETA wire formats exist in the wild:

    New (v0.3.0+): SGMETA[6] + length[4 big-endian] + JSON[length] + content
    Old (v0.1.x):  SGMETA[6] + � + JSON[scan to }] + content

  The download page's _extractMetadata only handled the new format. Transfers
  uploaded with the old format (vault snapshots, older send-upload.js) had their
  SGMETA envelope NOT stripped — the user received the raw payload including the
  SGMETA header, making it a corrupt file.

  The vault-snapshot.zip transfer at #help-aunt-2780 was uploaded with old
  send-upload.js and therefore used the old wire format. When downloaded via
  /en-gb/download/, the file started with:
      SGMETA�{"filename": "vault-snapshot.zip"}PK...
  instead of:
      PK...

Fix (a28e8206):
  Detect the null-byte discriminator (bytes[6] === 0x00) to identify old-format
  payloads, then brace-count to find the end of the JSON metadata before content.

Bug test pattern:
  PASSES while the bug exists (old-format download starts with SGMETA).
  FAILS when fixed (download is clean, starts with file content) → delete this
  file and add a positive assertion to the main download test suite.

tests.doc:
BUG: Old-format transfer download starts with SGMETA (envelope not stripped).

        PASSES while the bug exists (SGMETA present in download).
        FAILS when fixed (SGMETA stripped, clean content delivered) → delete this file.

BUG: Old-format download does not deliver clean file content.

        PASSES while the bug exists (content not equal to original plaintext).
        FAILS when fixed (content IS the original plaintext) → delete this file.
